title: Scratch and Block Coding: A Gentle Start to Programming
lead: Block-based coding removes syntax errors and lets kids focus on logic. Scratch is free, widely used, and a natural stepping stone to text-based languages.

sections.title: Why Block Coding First
content: No typos, no missing semicolons. Kids snap blocks together and see results immediately. The concepts—loops, conditionals, variables—transfer directly to Python or JavaScript later.

sections.title: What Kids Can Build
content: Simple games, animated stories, quizzes. Scratch's sprite and backdrop library makes it easy to create something that feels real. Sharing projects online adds motivation.

sections.title: When to Move to Text
content: When they've made a few projects and start feeling limited by blocks—or when they ask "how do real programmers do it?"—that's the time to introduce Python or JavaScript.

cta.title: Guide the Transition to Code
cta.text: GuruForU tutors can help kids move from Scratch to text-based programming.

faq.question: Why start with block coding instead of text?
faq.answer: No typos or missing semicolons. Kids snap blocks together and see results immediately. Loops, conditionals, variables—the concepts transfer to Python or JavaScript later.

faq.question: What can kids build with Scratch?
faq.answer: Simple games, animated stories, quizzes. Scratch's sprite and backdrop library makes it easy to create something that feels real. Sharing projects online adds motivation.

faq.question: When should kids move from Scratch to text-based programming?
faq.answer: When they've made a few projects and feel limited by blocks, or when they ask 'how do real programmers do it?'—that's the time for Python or JavaScript.
